title: Selected Psalms — Guarding the Tongue and Trust in God

sections.opening: Sit, my child, the candle is lit and the heart is ready. In the previous lesson we met the first chapters of the Book of Psalms — beginnings, success and protection. Today we'll delve into psalms that deal with challenges each of us knows: how to guard your tongue, how to face pursuers and how to find trust when the world seems threatening. Chapters 7-12 in Psalms are a practical guide for dealing with the most basic human challenges. Each chapter gives concrete tools for daily coping. King David didn't write philosophy here — he wrote life wisdom, because he himself faced the same difficulties.
sections.theoryA:
How do Psalms 7-12 help in personal crisis situations?

Chapters 7-12 in Psalms are a spiritual "emergency kit" for crisis situations. Chapter 7 provides protection from pursuers and justice in judgment, while chapter 12 protects from evil speech and gossip.

Chapter 7 — "Protection from pursuers" — this is the chapter you say when someone is pursuing you, threatening you or trying to harm you. Its virtue is well-known: protection against enemies and pursuers, and winning in righteous judgment. The inner message is deep: "God judges righteously — whoever is pursued will merit true judgment." This isn't just prayer, it's a declaration of faith that justice ultimately wins.

Chapter 8 brings "grace and kindness" — a virtue for finding favor in people's eyes and success in business. Its message is moving: "How majestic is Your name — man was created in God's image, with tremendous power." It reminds us of the greatness within us, which sometimes we forget.

Chapter 9 is special for healing sick children — "Healing a sick child." Every mother who has experienced this fear knows how powerful this chapter is. Its virtue is for healing a sick child and thanksgiving for miracles, and the message: "I will thank God with all my heart — gratitude brings more goodness."

Chapter 10 deals with inner fear — "Nullifying fear and evil spirits." It gives confidence against evil spirit and fear. Its message: "Why do You stand far off, God — even when God seems distant, He is close." This is the chapter for moments when fear takes over.

practice1.instruction: Choose a situation in your life that causes you anxiety or fear. Read Psalms chapter 10 aloud, and after each verse stop and ask yourself: How do these words relate to my situation? Write one sentence about the connection between the verse and your situation.

sections.theoryB:
What is the secret meaning of Psalms 11-12 for guarding the tongue?

Chapters 11-12 in Psalms deal with the power of speech and protection from evil speech. Chapter 11 gives confidence against the wicked, and chapter 12 is the strongest shield against evil speech and gossip.

Chapter 11 — "Trust against the wicked" — this is the chapter you say when evil people threaten you. Its virtue is against fear of the wicked and evil people, and the message is sharp: "In God I take refuge — when the wicked threaten, God is the shield." This isn't passivity, it's active trust.

Chapter 12 is the strongest chapter against evil speech. Its virtue: "Against evil speech and gossip, guarding the tongue." The message is deep: "Save, God, for the pious are gone — guard your tongue and you will merit kindness." This is a direct connection between guarding the tongue and receiving kindness from above.

There is a special connection between these chapters and the months of the year. Chapter 22 corresponds to the month of Adar (sign of Pisces), and chapter 27 to the month of Elul (sign of Virgo). This deepens the connection to special spiritual times in the year.

The tradition of the Baal Shem Tov adds another layer: each person says the chapter corresponding to their age plus 1. So a 30-year-old would say chapter 31, and a 25-year-old would say chapter 26. This creates a personal connection between your age and the appropriate psalms.

practice2.instruction: Think of a situation where someone spoke evil speech or gossip about you. Read Psalms chapter 12 three times. The first time — ask for protection. The second time — ask forgiveness for the one who spoke. The third time — give thanks for the strength to guard your tongue. Write in one sentence how you feel after the exercise.

sections.deepDive:
The secret not written in books: Reading psalms according to the letters of the name

There is a wonderful virtue that the Chida reveals in his books, and only few know about it: reading verses from Psalms 119 according to the letters of your name. The Chida says this is "an extremely wonderful virtue."

Psalms 119 is the longest chapter in Psalms — 176 verses, divided into 22 groups of 8 verses, according to the order of the Hebrew alphabet. Each group begins with a different letter from the Hebrew alphabet. Each letter has 8 special verses.

How does it work? You take the letters of your name, and for each letter you read the corresponding 8 verses. For example: the name Sarah — Shin, Resh, Heh. So you read verses 161-168 (Shin), verses 153-160 (Resh), and verses 33-40 (Heh).

Why is this so powerful? Because it creates a direct connection between your personal name and the holy words of King David. It's as if the psalms were written especially for you, with your name within them.

The tradition says this brings personal salvation tailored exactly to your soul. This isn't a substitute for regular psalm reading, but a special addition that gives unique spiritual power to your name.

questions.question: Which chapter in Psalms is said for healing a sick child?
questions.explanation: Chapter 9 is the special chapter for healing a sick child, with the virtue 'for healing a sick child, thanksgiving for miracles'.

questions.question: According to the Baal Shem Tov's tradition, which psalm chapter would a 25-year-old say?
questions.explanation: According to the tradition, each person says the chapter corresponding to their age plus 1. A 25-year-old would say chapter 26.

questions.question: Which chapter in Psalms is strongest against evil speech?
questions.explanation: Chapter 12 is the strongest chapter against evil speech and gossip, with the virtue 'guarding the tongue'.

questions.question: How many verses are in Psalms 119?
questions.explanation: Psalms 119 is the longest chapter with 176 verses, divided into 22 groups of 8 verses according to the alphabet.

questions.question: What is the main message of Psalms chapter 8?
questions.explanation: Chapter 8 deals with grace and kindness, with the virtue 'for finding favor in people's eyes, success in business' and the message about human greatness.

closing.summary: Today we met psalms that are essential daily tools: protection from pursuers (chapter 7), finding grace (chapter 8), healing children (chapter 9), nullifying fear (chapter 10), trust against the wicked (chapter 11) and guarding the tongue (chapter 12). We learned about the special virtue of reading Psalms 119 according to the letters of the name, and about the connection between age and the appropriate chapter according to the Baal Shem Tov.
closing.wisdom: Whoever trusts in God and guards their tongue — merits kindness from above.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we'll meet Psalms 13-18 — how to deal with despair and how to find salvation in the most difficult times.

faq.question: How do you know which psalm chapter to read in a specific situation?
faq.answer: Each chapter has special topics and virtues. Chapter 7 for pursuers, chapter 9 for sick children, chapter 12 against evil speech. Choose according to your situation.

faq.question: What is the virtue of reading psalms according to the letters of the name?
faq.answer: This is based on Psalms 119, where each Hebrew letter has 8 verses. You read the verses corresponding to the letters of your name for personal salvation.

faq.question: Must psalms be read in Hebrew?
faq.answer: Hebrew is the original and most powerful language, but translation also has power. The essence is intention and heart, not just language.

faq.question: When is it best to read psalms?
faq.answer: There's no wrong time for psalms. In the morning for strength, in the evening for inner peace, and in times of trouble for salvation. Psalms work at any hour.

seo.title: Selected Psalms: Chapters 7-12 for Guarding the Tongue and Trust
seo.description: Learn how Psalms 7-12 protect from pursuers and evil speech. Special virtues for healing children, finding grace and guarding the tongue. Reading according to name letters.
